sb_prompt: HOW DO YOU FEEL ABOUT THE PRACTICE OF SOME COMPANIES OR GOVERNMENT AGENCIES TESTING EMPLOYEES OR PROSPECTIVE EMPLOYEES FOR DRUGS?  IS RANDOM SPOT TESTING JUSTIFIED?  WHAT LIMITS SHOULD THERE BE
raw_output:
ParticipantA: Hi, is this the company that I interviewed with last week?
ParticipantB: Yes, it is. How can I help you?
ParticipantA: I was just wondering about the drug testing process. I have a drug test scheduled for tomorrow and I'm a little nervous.
ParticipantB: That's completely understandable. Our drug testing process is pretty standard. We use a urine test to detect any illicit drugs or prescription medications that may affect your work.
ParticipantA: Okay, that sounds good. I haven't used any drugs in years, but I did recently get a prescription for antidepressants. Will that show up in my drug test?
ParticipantB: Yes, it's possible that your prescription medication could show up in your drug test. However, we do allow for certain prescription medications as long as they are within the therapeutic range.
ParticipantA: Okay, that's good to know. I just wanted to make sure that I wasn't going to fail the test for something that I couldn't help.
ParticipantB: Absolutely, we understand that people have different medical needs and we try to accommodate that as much as possible. Just make sure that you bring your prescription with you to the test so that we can verify that it's within the therapeutic range.
ParticipantA: Okay, I will do that. Thank you so much for your help.
ParticipantB: You're welcome, and good luck on your drug test tomorrow!
